response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and systems software developer with a strong background in web, mobile, and embedded software, but his profile has essentially no overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His closest relevant experience is low-latency application development at Sociomantic using DLang, but that is application-layer performance work, not silicon or accelerator architecture. His skills list (TypeScript, MongoDB, Docker, etc.) confirms a software application focus entirely disconnected from the hardware and AI workload domains this role requires.
strengths: Over a decade of software engineering experience across diverse technical domains, satisfying the BS+8yr experience threshold
Demonstrated performance-conscious engineering at Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/second with low-latency, async I/O design
Exposure to embedded systems and hardware-adjacent work (robotic arms, NI LabWindows C/C++, wave field synthesis thesis) shows some breadth beyond pure web development
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling experience of any kind — the primary technical requirement of the role is completely absent
Zero background in AI/deep-learning workloads, GPU architecture, or neural network training/inference optimization
Hardware-software co-design and SIMD/MIMD architecture experience are entirely missing; the candidate's skill set is exclusively at the application and middleware layer

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP modeling or hardware architecture experience
No AI/ML or deep-learning workload expertise
